item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-018","score":1,"pass_criteria_met":false,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response correctly rejects the accuracy-only conclusion, treats the human 3-point accuracy edge as potentially real, and proposes a hybrid routing architecture with automation for high-confidence/low-risk cases and humans for sensitive/uncertain cases. However, it does not explicitly frame the key SELA comparison as an order-of-magnitude gap in unit cost, throughput, and scalability; it only mentions cost, speed, scale, and latency qualitatively. So it is a solid partial wakeup, not a full pass."}
